actionLibraryList.title: Tabletop Thoracic Rotation
actionLibraryList.context: During the rotation, avoid rushing the movement; maintain slow, controlled motion to prevent overstretching or misalignment.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523622522.jpg","context":"1.Raise one arm with the elbow bent and place your hand behind your head."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523625921.jpg","context":"1.During the rotation, keep your hand gently touching or lightly supporting your head.\n2.Keep your pelvis and hips stable without moving.\n3.Position your knees hip-width apart."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during the rotation to help relax your body.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: At the maximum range, hold for one to two seconds, feel the stretch in your thoracic spine, and keep your chest open.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: During thoracic spine rotation, keep your hips from rotating.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Reverse Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and position the barbell across your upper back with a wide grip.
2.Inhale to prepare, then step backward with one leg and bend both knees to lower into a lunge position.
3.Keep your front knee aligned with your toes and lower your rear knee toward the ground without touching it.
4.Exhale and drive through your front leg and glutes to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the barbell to the floor position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, keep your shoulders back and down, engage your core.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Step back, bend both knees to lower your body into a lunge, then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Perform the movement slowly and with control, keeping your core engaged to maintain balance.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: It targets the glutes, quads, and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751507884618.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2.Pull your shoulders back and down.
3.Engage your core."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751507889315.jpg","context":"1.Keep your knee just above the floor.
2.Keep your torso straight."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: It targets the glutes, quads, and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the knee from collapsing inward or extending too far forward.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Stiff-Leg Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grip the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
2.Engage your core, retract your shoulder blades, and keep your back straight.
3.Hinge forward from the hips, maintaining a straight line between your torso and legs.
4.Lower the barbell slowly along the front of your thighs to mid-shin or your stretch limit.
5.Drive through your glutes and hamstrings to pull back to the starting position with control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749438751670.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell with both hands in a neutral grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749438774602.jpg","context":"1.Keep your back straight and control the movement by slightly bending your knees and pushing your hips back.\n2.Lean your torso forward, lowering the barbell along your legs to mid-calf or until you reach your maximum stretch."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower the barbell, exhale as you rise back up.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees stable—avoid forward travel or excessive bending.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Barbell Calf Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.tand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with a neutral grip.
2.Engage your core and, in a controlled manner, raise your heels by pushing through the ankles until you reach the highest point.
3.Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds, keeping your calves fully contracted.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934010205.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, grip the barbell with an overhand grip, engage your core, and keep your torso upright."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934014024.jpg","context":"1.With control, lift your heels off the ground and rise onto your toes as high as possible, squeezing your calves."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Control the movement speed and avoid using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Incline Dual-Handle Chest Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the bench to a 30° incline and lie back.
2.Keep chest up, core tight, shoulders depressed, and grip the handles.
3.Press the handles up with the chest, arms nearly straight, until above the clavicles.
4.Pause briefly at the top, feeling the upper chest contract.
5.Inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Elbows slightly below shoulder level.\n2.Neutral spine position.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748937805654.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748937810675.jpg","context":"1.Keep the forearms perpendicular to the floor.\n2.Keep the back pressed against the incline bench."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale to press up, inhale to lower down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the chest to press the handles up above the clavicles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the elbows from flaring out too wide.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Single-Leg Pallof Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the cable anchor roughly at chest height.
2.Stand on one leg with the other slightly raised, core engaged.
3.Grip the handles and hold them in front of your chest, elbows close to your sides.
4.Push the handles forward horizontally with control, keeping your arms slightly bent.
5.Pause briefly, then slowly return to the starting position in front of your chest.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750745585635.jpg","context":"1.Grip the handles and hold them in front of your chest.\n2.Stand on one leg, with the other leg slightly raised."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750745589620.jpg","context":"1.Push the handles forward from your chest along a horizontal line."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you push, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your core tight throughout, preventing any body sway or rotation.

actionLibraryList.title: Side Plank Hip Abduction
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Side-lying on the mat, support yourself on your forearm with the elbow directly under your shoulder.
2.Legs extended and stacked, with the top hand on your hip or resting naturally by your side.
3.Keep your pelvis stable and slowly lift the top leg straight to hip abduction range.
4.Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749807272217.jpg","context":"1.Support yourself on your forearm with the elbow directly under the shoulder.\n2.Keep your body in a straight line, maintaining a neutral pelvis."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749807276969.jpg","context":"1.Slowly lift the top leg straight up to hip abduction range."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on feeling the glutes engage.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid leaning forward or arching backward.
